content: Phase 3 of the North Frisian Medieval Wreck Survey is officially under way at Site NF-7 off Husum. The team has mobilised aboard the Nordkai vessel and completed the first dive briefings. More dispatches to follow as the season progresses.
excerpt: Phase 3 of the North Frisian survey begins at Site NF-7 - read more inside.
title: Phase 3 begins at Site NF-7

content: A short guide to how we plan a survey dive on the wreck site: tide windows, visibility checks, baseline measurements and the recording grid. Nils Johansen walks through the morning routine before the divers go in.
excerpt: How we plan a survey dive at NF-7 - read more inside.
title: How we plan a survey dive

content: Three finds worth sharing from this week's dives: a section of clinker-built hull planking, a fragment of glazed Hanseatic pottery, and an iron fitting still in situ. Each one tells us a little more about the vessel and its trade.
excerpt: Three finds worth sharing from NF-7 - read more inside.
title: Three finds worth sharing

content: A look at the season ahead: our survey plan for Phase 3 runs June through August, with timber sampling, photogrammetry of the exposed structure, and a public open day in Husum towards the end of the field season.
excerpt: Our plan for the Phase 3 field season - read more inside.
title: The Phase 3 field season plan

content: Field notes from the team at NF-7. Conditions have been mixed this week - a strong northerly cut two dives short - but the recording grid is now fully laid out and the first photogrammetry pass of the exposed hull is complete.
excerpt: Field notes from the team at NF-7 - read more inside.
title: Field notes from the team

content: How we conserve waterlogged timber once it leaves the seabed. A draft explainer on the first steps - keeping samples wet, documentation, and handover to the Hamburg Maritime Museum for stabilisation. Still being checked before we publish.
excerpt: Conserving waterlogged timber - first steps - read more inside.
title: Conserving waterlogged timber: first steps
